summary: The number of needles DSNY collects mostly reflects how many happen to turn up during routine street and lot cleanups, so it moves up and down with incidental discovery rather than a change in policy. DSNY's own reporting attributes the sharp FY26 drop (down 71% in the year's first four months) directly to fewer needles being found during cleanings, not to any pullback in outreach or cleaning effort.

sources.label: DSNY, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report
